first_paragraph: The **pale-faced miner bee**[[1]](#cite_note-commonname-1) (*Andrena pallidifovea*) is a species of [miner bee](/wiki/Mining_bee "Mining bee") in the family [Andrenidae](/wiki/Andrenidae "Andrenidae"). Another common name for this species is the pale-fovea andrena. It is found in Central America and North America.[[2]](#cite_note-itis-2)[[3]](#cite_note-gbif-3)[[4]](#cite_note-buglink-4)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Species of bee

| *Andrena pallidifovea* | |

| Kingdom: | [Animalia](/wiki/Animal "Animal") |
| Phylum: | [Arthropoda](/wiki/Arthropod "Arthropod") |
| Class: | [Insecta](/wiki/Insect "Insect") |
| Order: | [Hymenoptera](/wiki/Hymenoptera "Hymenoptera") |
| Family: | [Andrenidae](/wiki/Andrenidae "Andrenidae") |
| Genus: | [*Andrena*](/wiki/Andrena "Andrena") |

The **pale-faced miner bee**[[1]](#cite_note-commonname-1) (*Andrena pallidifovea*) is a species of [miner bee](/wiki/Mining_bee "Mining bee") in the family [Andrenidae](/wiki/Andrenidae "Andrenidae"). Another common name for this species is the pale-fovea andrena. It is found in Central America and North America.[[2]](#cite_note-itis-2)[[3]](#cite_note-gbif-3)[[4]](#cite_note-buglink-4)
